scope.purpose: Batch seed input for Mimo or read-only scripts to produce review-ready knowledge thread packs.
outOfScope: X Search or Grok file changes

selection.rationale: Agentic Coding is closest to the Loop Engineering sample and should validate direct replication first. Context Engineering should run second because it tests a method-level topic with broader source boundaries. MCP runs third because it is a strong protocol/ecosystem topic, but it depends on the context/tool-use framing that the first two topics establish.

sourceRequirements.companyStrategyContextPolicy: Company strategy sources may be kept only as optional company_strategy_context targets and must not count toward topic readiness.

topics.title: Agentic Coding
topics.definitionDraft: A software-development mode where an AI agent plans, edits, runs tools, verifies results, and iterates with a developer or automation system across real repositories.
topics.whyNowDraft: Claude Code, Codex-style agents, GitHub workflows, and real-repo benchmarks have moved AI coding from completion assistance toward reviewable multi-step engineering loops.

signal.query: "agentic coding" Claude Code Boris Cherny workflow
signal.notes: Use only as freshness or vocabulary signal.
Prefer practitioner posts or conference talk pages with stable URLs over raw X if possible.

official_definition.query: Anthropic Claude Code overview official docs agentic coding
OpenAI Codex coding agent official docs
GitHub Copilot coding agent official docs pull request issues

transcript_context.query: Boris Cherny Claude Code podcast transcript coding agents
transcript_context.notes: Transcript text must be captured before extracting quotes.

transcript_context.query: Claude Code team interview transcript agentic coding
transcript_context.notes: Use only transcript or reliable written interview text.

paper_foundation.query: SWE-agent agent computer interfaces automated software engineering paper
ReAct reasoning acting language models paper tool use coding agents

implementation_signal.query: Claude Code SDK hooks subagents skills official docs

mustHaveSources.title: Claude Code overview
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Defines the official coding-agent product surface.

mustHaveSources.title: Claude Code: Best practices for agentic coding
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Primary official explanation of workflow, context, verification, and iteration.

mustHaveSources.title: anthropics/claude-code-action
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Shows agentic coding entering issue and pull-request workflows.

mustHaveSources.title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Grounds readiness in real repository issue resolution.

mustHaveSources.title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Connects agent interfaces to automated software engineering tasks.

weakSpots: The term overlaps with Loop Engineering; keep this page focused on the broader software-development mode, not only repeated Claude Code workflow loops.
Vendor docs can overclaim capability; pair official sources with benchmarks and implementation examples.
Transcript candidates must expose actual transcript text before page-ready quotes.
Avoid turning GitHub Copilot, Codex, or Claude Code marketing pages into unverified performance claims.

edgeIdeas.evidenceNeed: Map product workflow claims to SWE-bench or SWE-agent evaluation scope.
Connect Claude Code workflows to GitHub Action or SDK implementation.
Use transcript explanation to clarify, not replace, official definitions.
Show how real-repo benchmarks pressure implementation choices such as tests, patches, and review loops.

companyStrategyContextTargets.reason: Optional company page context for GitHub Copilot strategy; do not count earnings or IR sources toward topic readiness.
Optional company page context for Codex product strategy; official product docs may count, IR-style strategy sources may not.

topics.handoffNotes: This is the safest first Mimo batch topic because Loop Engineering already supplies overlapping Claude Code, SWE-bench, and implementation-source patterns.

topics.title: Context Engineering
topics.definitionDraft: The practice of designing what information, memory, tools, retrieval results, instructions, and state an AI agent sees at each step so it can act reliably over long or complex tasks.
topics.whyNowDraft: As agents move from one-shot prompts to long-running work, context becomes an engineered runtime resource rather than a single prompt.

signal.notes: Use signal sources for vocabulary emergence only.
Separate practitioner vocabulary from official product docs.

official_definition.query: Anthropic prompt engineering overview context official docs
Claude Code memory context official docs
OpenAI Agents SDK context management official docs

transcript_context.query: agent context management interview transcript LLM memory retrieval
transcript_context.notes: Prefer long-form interviews with transcript text and named speakers.

transcript_context.query: Claude Code context memory workflow transcript
transcript_context.notes: Use transcript only to explain practice and tradeoffs.

paper_foundation.query: Lost in the Middle context window paper
Retrieval-Augmented Generation knowledge intensive NLP tasks paper
MemGPT memory management LLM agents paper

implementation_signal.query: Claude Code memory custom slash commands docs
MCP resources prompts tools context official docs

mustHaveSources.title: Prompt engineering overview
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Anchors context work in official prompt and instruction design guidance.

mustHaveSources.title: Building effective agents
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Explains agents as systems that combine models, tools, workflows, and context.

mustHaveSources.title: Claude Code memory
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Concrete implementation surface for reusable project and user context.

mustHaveSources.title: Lost in the Middle: How Language Models Use Long Contexts
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Shows why longer context does not automatically mean better context.

mustHaveSources.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Foundation for retrieval as a context supply mechanism.

mustHaveSources.title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Foundation for explicit memory management in long-running agent tasks.

weakSpots: The phrase is practitioner vocabulary and may not have one official definition; page definition must say what the product means by it.
Do not collapse Context Engineering into prompt tips. Include retrieval, memory, tools, instructions, state, compression, and verification.
Paper sources can be broad; Mimo should explain exactly how each paper supports the topic.
Implementation examples should prove context handling, not just mention a larger context window.

edgeIdeas.evidenceNeed: Connect long-context failure modes to official memory, prompt, or retrieval guidance.
Show how memory files, resources, prompts, or SDK context objects make guidance operational.
Use practitioner vocabulary only after grounding it in retrieval, memory, or long-context research.
Explain how implementation choices address position bias, context bloat, or stale memory.

companyStrategyContextTargets.reason: Optional company page context for Claude memory and agent platform direction; technical docs count, financial strategy sources do not.
Optional company page context for Agents platform direction; product docs may count, IR-style strategy sources may not.

topics.handoffNotes: Give this to Mimo after Agentic Coding so the reviewer can compare a product/workflow topic with a method/runtime topic.

topics.title: MCP
topics.definitionDraft: Model Context Protocol is an open protocol for connecting AI applications to tools, data sources, prompts, and resources through standardized client-server integrations.
topics.whyNowDraft: Agentic products increasingly need reliable tool and data access, and MCP is becoming a common integration surface across clients, servers, SDKs, and examples.

signal.query: "Model Context Protocol" adoption client server tools
signal.notes: Prefer official ecosystem posts or stable engineering blog posts.
Use community posts only as adoption signals.

official_definition.query: Model Context Protocol official docs introduction

transcript_context.query: Model Context Protocol interview transcript Anthropic
transcript_context.notes: Do not use video title alone; require transcript text.

transcript_context.query: MCP protocol tools agents podcast transcript
transcript_context.notes: Use for motivation and ecosystem context only.

paper_foundation.query: Toolformer language models external tools paper
ReAct reasoning acting language models tools paper
Tool learning with foundation models survey paper

mustHaveSources.title: Model Context Protocol documentation
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Primary official entry point for the protocol.

mustHaveSources.title: modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Canonical specification source for protocol terms and capabilities.

mustHaveSources.title: modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Official SDK surface for client/server implementation examples.

mustHaveSources.title: modelcontextprotocol/python-sdk
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Second official SDK surface to prove cross-language implementation.

mustHaveSources.title: Connect Claude Code to tools via MCP
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Product-level official source for MCP inside Claude Code workflows.

mustHaveSources.title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
mustHaveSources.whyRequired: Tool-use foundation for why a protocolized tool layer matters.

weakSpots: MCP is a protocol topic, not a generic tool-use topic. Keep protocol primitives, client/server shape, resources, prompts, tools, security, and SDKs visible.
Community server lists can be noisy; prioritize official spec, SDKs, reference servers, and product docs.
Transcript sources may be sparse; if transcript coverage is thin, mark the pack thin rather than inventing motivation.
Do not count company adoption announcements or earnings remarks as technical readiness.

edgeIdeas.evidenceNeed: Map spec capabilities to TypeScript or Python SDK implementation.
Connect MCP docs to Claude Code MCP docs without making Claude Code the whole protocol.
Explain how tool-use research motivates standardized tool/data access.
Use SDK/reference-server examples to vet ecosystem adoption signals.

companyStrategyContextTargets.reason: Optional company page context for MCP launch and ecosystem strategy; technical docs and spec count, financial strategy sources do not.

topics.handoffNotes: MCP is ready for Mimo after the reviewer verifies current official doc URLs and transcript availability.

mimoInstructions.quotePolicy: Short quotes only; rendered page should prefer paraphrase and preserve source binding.
mimoInstructions.financialSourcePolicy: Reject earnings_transcript, sec_filing, investor_relations, annual_report, and shareholder_letter as readiness sources. Preserve only in companyStrategyContextTargets with excludedFromTopicReadiness=true.
mimoInstructions.thinPackRule: If a required role is missing, transcript text is unavailable, or source count is below 15, mark the topic thin.
